faq.question: Why are Dyson error codes such as `51C2` logged as unknown or missing from the smart home interface?
faq.answer: Warnings such as `Received unknown error code: 51C2` occur when a device reports a status code that has not yet been catalogued by the plugin. While these codes are added to the plugin's internal database as they are identified to reduce log noise, they are not currently exposed as actionable faults in Matter-compatible apps. This is a limitation of the **Matter 1.4 specification**, which lacks a standardised method for air purifiers to report granular hardware faults. Future updates to the Matter protocol may allow these codes to be properly mapped and displayed.
faq.semantic_abstract: Dyson air purifier hardware status codes (e.g. `51C2`) are logged by the plugin but remain unmapped to Matter attributes because the Matter 1.4 specification lacks a standardised fault-reporting cluster for this device category. This limitation prevents granular hardware errors from being exposed to Matter controllers even when the plugin correctly identifies the status code from the hardware's MQTT stream.
